summary: Drew DeVault publicly announces the Hare programming language, which he had been teasing as a 'secret programming language' for several months. The post itself is a brief redirect to the official Hare blog for details. Hare is a systems programming language designed as a simpler alternative to C, emphasizing simplicity, stability, and manual memory management. The announcement marks the transition from private development to public visibility for the project.
key_points: Hare is officially revealed as the 'secret programming language' DeVault had been hinting at for months
The announcement is deliberately minimal, directing readers to the official Hare blog for substance
The project had been developed privately before this public unveiling
money_quotes: The "secret programming language" I have been teasing for several months now is finally here!
It is called Hare, and you can read about it on the Hare blog.
key_insight: After months of private development and public teasing, DeVault unveils Hare as his vision for a new systems programming language.
